# SECTION 5: KTU 2024 Scheme Examination Question Bank & Topic Recap

> [!IMPORTANT]
> All questions are framed in the **KTU 2024 Scheme ESE pattern**: Part A (3 marks), Part B (14 marks) with sub-parts and internal choice.

---

## 5.1 PART A — Short Answer Questions (3 Marks Each)

### Question A1
**[KTU University Exam – June 2024]**
> **Define Leaching Requirement. An irrigation water has ECiw = 1.2 dS/m. For a crop requiring ECdw = 4.8 dS/m, calculate the LR and state whether the water needs blending.**

**Model Answer:**

> **Leaching Requirement (LR)** is the minimum fraction (or %) of the irrigation water that must drain *through* the root zone to keep soil salinity below the crop-tolerance threshold.
>
> **Calculation:**
>
> $$LR = \frac{EC_{iw}}{EC_{dw}} \times 100 = \frac{1.2}{4.8} \times 100 = 25\%$$
>
> **Decision:** Since LR = 25% is within the typical range (10–30%) for sustainable irrigation, the water is acceptable **without blending**, provided a leaching schedule of 25% extra water is followed.

---

### Question A2
**[KTU University Exam – December 2023]**
> **Compare mole drains with pipe drains. Under what soil conditions is each preferred?**

**Model Answer:**

| Feature | Mole Drains | Pipe Drains |
|---|---|---|
| Construction | Drawn by a mole plough | Trench + pipe laying |
| Soil type | **Heavy clay (Ip > 25)** | Sandy, loamy, mixed |
| Lifespan | **3–8 years** | **30–50 years** |
| Cost | Low initial | Higher initial |
| Spacing | **1.5–4 m** | **20–50 m** |
| Depth | **0.4–0.6 m** | **0.8–1.5 m** |
| Maintenance | Re-ploughing | Envelope cleaning |

> **Mole drains** are preferred in **heavy, swelling clay soils** with high plasticity and no stones. **Pipe drains** are preferred in **loamy, sandy, or mixed soils** where long-term performance is needed.

---

## 5.2 PART B — Long Answer Questions (14 Marks, with Choice)

> [!NOTE]
> Each 14-mark question has **two sub-parts of 7 marks each**. Internal choice is provided.

---

### Question B1 (Set 1, Choice A) — Drainage System Design

**[KTU University Exam – June 2024 / KTU Model Paper 2024]**

> **(a)** Explain the various types of **surface drainage systems** with neat sketches. Discuss the **design criteria** for an open-ditch surface drain in a humid tropical region.
>
> **(b)** A surface drain of trapezoidal cross-section has bottom width **1.5 m**, side slopes **1:1**, depth of flow **0.8 m**, bed slope **0.0008**, and Manning's **n = 0.030**. Compute the **discharge** it can carry.

**Model Solution (a) — 7 marks:**

> **Types of Surface Drainage Systems (3 marks):**
>
> 1. **Land Grading** – uniform slope by earth-moving.
> 2. **Random (Natural) Drains** – natural depressions, swales.
> 3. **Parallel Drains** – parallel field drains → single main.
> 4. **Interception Drains** – across slope to capture runoff.
> 5. **Bedding Systems** – raised beds with furrow drains.
>
> **Design Criteria (4 marks):**
>
> - **Drainage coefficient** of 10–25 mm/day (humid).
> - **Return period** of 5–10 years.
> - **Channel slope** ≥ 0.05% to avoid silting.
> - **Channel side slopes** 1.5:1 to 2:1 (stable).
> - **Freeboard** ≥ 0.2 m.
> - **Velocity limits** — non-erosive (< 1.5 m/s in earth, < 2.5 m/s in lined).
> - **Manning's n** chosen based on lining.

**Model Solution (b) — 7 marks:**

**Step 1 — Cross-section area**

$$A = (b + zy) \cdot y = (1.5 + 1 \times 0.8) \times 0.8 = 2.3 \times 0.8 = 1.84 \text{ m}^{2}$$

**Step 2 — Wetted perimeter**

$$P = b + 2y\sqrt{1+z^{2}} = 1.5 + 2(0.8)\sqrt{2} = 1.5 + 1.6 \times 1.4142 = 1.5 + 2.263 = 3.763 \text{ m}$$

**Step 3 — Hydraulic radius**

$$R = \frac{A}{P} = \frac{1.84}{3.763} = 0.489 \text{ m}$$

**Step 4 — Manning's equation**

$$Q = \frac{1}{n} A R^{2/3} S^{1/2}$$

$$R^{2/3} = (0.489)^{2/3} = 0.625$$

$$S^{1/2} = \sqrt{0.0008} = 0.02828$$

$$Q = \frac{1}{0.030} \times 1.84 \times 0.625 \times 0.02828$$

$$Q = 33.33 \times 0.03255 = 1.085 \text{ m}^{3}/\text{s}$$

**Answer (b):** **Q ≈ 1.08 m³/s.**

---

### Question B2 (Set 1, Choice B) — Subsurface Drainage Design

**[KTU University Exam – December 2023 / KTU Model Paper 2024]**

> **(a)** What are the **components of a subsurface drainage system**? Explain **pipe materials** used with their relative merits.
>
> **(b)** A sub-surface drainage system is to be designed for a loamy soil field with the following data:
> - **K = 1.5 m/day**
> - **d_e = 3.5 m**
> - **m = 0.4 m** (midpoint water-table height)
> - **q = 0.010 m/day**
>
> Compute the **drain spacing L** using **Hooghoudt's steady-state equation**.

**Model Solution (a) — 7 marks:**

> **Components (3 marks):**
>
> 1. **Laterals** – small perforated pipes collecting water.
> 2. **Submains** – collect from laterals.
> 3. **Main / Collector** – carries to outfall.
> 4. **Manholes / Junction Boxes** – inspection, sediment removal.
> 5. **Envelope / Filter** – sand, gravel, geotextile around pipe.
> 6. **Outlet** – controlled discharge structure.
>
> **Pipe Materials (4 marks):**
>
> | Material | Merits | Demerits |
> |---|---|---|
> | **Clay tile** | Low cost, inert | Brittle, short length, joint leakage |
> | **Concrete** | Strong, locally available | Heavy, sulphate attack in acidic soils |
> | **Corrugated PVC** | Flexible, long life, easy install | UV-degraded if uncovered |
> | **HDPE** | Chemically inert, very flexible | Higher cost, buckling under deep fill |
> | **Bituminized fibre** | Light, smooth interior | Outdated, limited strength |

**Model Solution (b) — 7 marks:**

**Step 1 — Identify parameters**

$$K = 1.5, \quad d_e = 3.5, \quad m = 0.4, \quad q = 0.010$$

**Step 2 — Hooghoudt's equation**

$$L = \sqrt{\frac{4Km^{2} + 8K d_e m}{q}}$$

**Step 3 — Numerator terms**

$$4Km^{2} = 4 \times 1.5 \times 0.16 = 0.96$$

$$8K d_e m = 8 \times 1.5 \times 3.5 \times 0.4 = 16.8$$

**Step 4 — Sum and divide**

$$\text{Numerator} = 0.96 + 16.8 = 17.76$$

$$\frac{17.76}{0.010} = 1776$$

**Step 5 — Square root**

$$L = \sqrt{1776} = 42.14 \text{ m}$$

**Answer (b):** **L ≈ 42 m** (practically rounded to **40 m**).

---

### Question B3 (Set 2, Choice A) — Leaching & Water Quality

**[KTU University Exam – June 2024]**

> **(a)** Define **Leaching Requirement (LR)**. Derive the relationship between LR, ECiw, and ECdw. Compute LR for **ECiw = 2.0 dS/m** and **ECdw = 8.0 dS/m**.
>
> **(b)** A farmer applies **800 mm** of irrigation per season. With LR = 25%, calculate the **extra water (m³/ha)** required for leaching. If the field area is **5 ha**, what is the **total volume of leaching water** per season?

**Model Solution (a) — 7 marks:**

> **Definition (1 mark):**
> LR is the fraction (%) of irrigation water that must drain through the root zone to flush out accumulated salts.
>
> **Derivation (3 marks):**
>
> Salt balance: **Vi · Ci = Vd · Cd**
>
> Let **LF = Vd / Vi**:
>
> $$LF = \frac{C_i}{C_d} = \frac{EC_{iw}}{EC_{dw}}$$
>
> $$LR\,(\%) = \frac{EC_{iw}}{EC_{dw}} \times 100$$
>
> **Calculation (3 marks):**
>
> $$LR = \frac{2.0}{8.0} \times 100 = 25\%$$

**Model Solution (b) — 7 marks:**

**Step 1 — Leaching depth**

$$d_{leach} = LR \times d_{irrigation} = 0.25 \times 800 = 200 \text{ mm} = 0.200 \text{ m}$$

**Step 2 — Volume per hectare**

$$V_{1ha} = 0.200 \times 10{,}000 = 2000 \text{ m}^{3}/\text{ha}$$

**Step 3 — Total for 5 ha**

$$V_{total} = 2000 \times 5 = 10{,}000 \text{ m}^{3}$$

**Answer (b):** **Leaching water = 2000 m³/ha**, **total = 10,000 m³ for 5 ha per season**.

### Question B4 (Set 2, Choice B) — Mole Drains & Drainage Wells

**[KTU University Exam – December 2023]**

> **(a)** What are **mole drains**? Describe the **conditions of soil and topography** under which they are adopted. Discuss their **design and limitations**.
>
> **(b)** Discuss the **types of drainage wells** with their applications in irrigation command areas.

**Model Solution (a) — 7 marks:**

> **Definition (1 mark):**
> Mole drains are **unlined, cylindrical subsurface channels** formed by dragging a torpedo-shaped mole plough through heavy clay soil at depths of 0.4–0.6 m.
>
> **Soil & Topography Conditions (2 marks):**
>
> 1. Heavy clay (>30% clay, Ip > 25).
> 2. Stable subsoil that does not swell excessively when wet.
> 3. Land slope ≥ 0.1% (1 in 1000).
> 4. Free of stones and gravel.
>
> **Design (2 marks):**
> - Depth 0.4–0.6 m, spacing 1.5–4 m.
> - Grade ≥ 0.1%.
> - Mole plough speed 2–4 km/h.
> - Re-plough every 3–8 years.
>
> **Limitations (2 marks):**
> 1. Short lifespan.
> 2. Not suitable in sandy/silty soils.
> 3. Failure in stony ground.
> 4. Re-ploughing cost.
> 5. Cannot be used in deep water-table regions.

**Model Solution (b) — 7 marks:**

> **Types of Drainage Wells (4 marks):**
>
> 1. **Shallow Open Wells** – for small farms; depth 5–10 m.
> 2. **Deep Tube Wells** – 50–200 m deep, with submersible pumps; for regional water-table control.
> 3. **Gravel-Packed Wells** – filter pack around screen for fine-sand aquifers.
> 4. **Recharge Wells** – reverse function; *inject* water into aquifer for storage.
> 5. **Relief Wells** – installed downstream of dams/cofferdams to relieve uplift pressure.
>
> **Applications (3 marks):**
> 1. **Coastal lowlands** (e.g., Kuttanad in Kerala) – lower saline water table.
> 2. **Command areas** with high post-monsoon water table.
> 3. **Construction sites** – temporary dewatering.
> 4. **Urban stormwater** management.

## 5.3 KTU Examiner's Valuation Warning & Common Pitfalls

> [!WARNING]
> **Where KTU students lose marks in Module 4:**
>
> 1. **Confusing `d` and `d_e`** — using actual depth **D** instead of equivalent depth **d_e** in Hooghoudt's formula (–2 marks).
> 2. **Unit mismatch in K, q, L** — KTU allows m/day for all, but mixing mm/day and m/day causes a 1000× error (–3 marks).
> 3. **Forgetting the `4Km²` term** — many students write only `8Kdm`, omitting `4Km²` (–2 marks).
> 4. **LR formula error** — using ECdw / ECiw instead of ECiw / ECdw (–3 marks). Always remember: *ECiw < ECdw*, so the ratio < 1, hence multiplied by 100 gives the **percentage**.
> 5. **Mole drain lifespan quoted as "permanent"** – incorrect; mark lost.
> 6. **Pipe material selection without justifying pH or soil texture** – full-marks answers *always* cite selection criteria.
> 7. **No sketch in surface-drainage questions** – lose at least 2 marks.

## 5.4 Topic Recap & Important Things to Remember (Rapid Revision Checklist)

> [!IMPORTANT]
> **Module 4 — Quick-Revision Bullet Bank**

### Surface Drainage
- ✅ Surface drainage removes water on the **soil surface**, not within the profile.
- ✅ Key types: **land grading, random, parallel, interception, bedding**.
- ✅ Design: **Drainage Coefficient (mm/day) + Manning's + Rational Method**.
- ✅ Velocity limits: **0.6–1.5 m/s (earth)**, **1.5–2.5 m/s (lined)**.
- ✅ Minimum slope: **0.05%** for self-cleaning.

### Subsurface Drainage
- ✅ Removes water **from the soil profile** via perforated pipes.
- ✅ Components: **laterals → submains → main → outfall**.
- ✅ Drain depth: **0.8–1.5 m**; spacing: **20–50 m**.
- ✅ Envelope: **sand, gravel, geotextile** to prevent piping.

### Hooghoudt's Equation (★ Most Important)
- ✅ Formula: $L = \sqrt{\dfrac{4Km^{2} + 8Kdm}{q}}$.
- ✅ Variables: **K, m, d, q** — all in consistent units.
- ✅ Use **d_e** (equivalent depth), not actual D.
- ✅ **Boundary**: drain *above* impervious layer.

### Donnan's Equation
- ✅ Special case: drain *on* the impervious layer.
- ✅ $L = \sqrt{\dfrac{4Kmd + 2Km^{2}}{q}}$.
- ✅ **Rare in KTU papers**; know the distinction.

### Pipe Materials
- ✅ Most common: **Corrugated PVC (CPP) and HDPE**.
- ✅ Selection criteria: **soil pH, texture, depth, cost, lifespan**.
- ✅ Diameter: **50–200 mm** for laterals; **150–300 mm** for mains.

### Mole Drains
- ✅ Soil: **heavy clay, Ip > 25**.
- ✅ Depth: **0.4–0.6 m**; spacing: **1.5–4 m**.
- ✅ Lifespan: **3–8 years**; re-ploughing needed.
- ✅ Not for **sandy/silty/stony soils**.

### Drainage Wells
- ✅ Vertical boreholes pumping water out of aquifer.
- ✅ Use **radius of influence + drawdown formula**.
- ✅ Common in **coastal Kuttanad (Kerala)**.

### Leaching Requirement
- ✅ Formula: $LR = \dfrac{EC_{iw}}{EC_{dw}} \times 100\,\%$.
- ✅ Always **ECiw < ECdw** (concentration by ET).
- ✅ FAO rule: **leach 10–15% if ECiw > 1.5 dS/m**.

### Water Quality
- ✅ **EC** for salinity; **SAR** for sodium.
- ✅ **RSC** for carbonate hazard; **pH** for acidity.
- ✅ **TDS ≈ 640 × EC** (mg/L).
- ✅ USDA classes **C1 to C5** (EC) and **S1 to S4** (SAR).

### Project Topics (KTU Final-Year Suggestions)
- ✅ Design of a farm drainage system for a Kerala village using GIS.
- ✅ Comparative study of mole vs. pipe drains in Kuttanad.
- ✅ Water-quality assessment of drainage effluent in a coconut-based farming system.
- ✅ Hooghoudt-based spacing optimization for a given soil series.
- ✅ Reuse potential of agricultural drainage water for irrigation.
- ✅ Salinity mapping of a coastal command using remote sensing.
